THE IDENTIFICATION OF EASTERLIN IN THE AMENDED INTERROGATORY RESPONSES, ABSENT ANY STATEMENT AS TO WHAT HE KNEW, IS INSUFFICIENT TO PRECLUDE SHIFTING THE BURDEN TO PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO PLAINTIFF EXHIBITS A AND D ARE SUSTAINED BASED ON HEARSAY. THE EASTERLIN DECLARATION, THEREFORE, IS NOT RELEVANT BECAUSE IT IS BASED ON THE HEARSAY DOCUMENTS AND IS DEVOID OF EVIDENCE OF ANY CONTACT WITH DECEDENT. THE BROWN DECLARATION IS ADMISSIBLE TO ESTABLISH DEFENDANT'S BOILERS WERE ON THE SHIPS BUT IT DOES NOT ESTABLISH EXPOSURE BY DECEDENT TO ASBESTOS. THE AY DECLARATION IS BASED ON INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY AND FOR THAT REASON, IT IS NOT ADMITTED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRESENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Andrew Chew of Brayton Purcell (898.1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Shelley Tinkoff of Brydon Hugo & Parker (808.0300) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on factually devoid discovery responses. The identification of Easterlin in the amended interrogatory responses, absent any statement as to what he knew, is insufficient to preclude shifting the burden to plaintiff. Defendant's objections to plaintiff exhibits A and D are sustained based on hearsay. The Easterlin declaration, therefore, is not relevant because it is based on the hearsay documents and is devoid of evidence of any contact with decedent. The Brown declaration is admissible to establish defendant's boilers were on the ships but it does not establish exposure by decedent to asbestos. The Ay declaration is based on inadmissible hearsay and for that reason, it is not admitted. Plaintiff has failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue of fact. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
